description_long: This shotgun features a robust, angular metal receiver with a matte finish and prominent vented slots along the sides for heat dissipation. The barrel assembly is short and wide, with a distinctive hexagonal muzzle and dual bores, suggesting a double-barrel configuration. The stock is crafted from wood with a natural grain texture, providing a classic contrast to the modern metallic body. A pronounced trigger guard and a visible trigger are present, with the guard extending in a squared, elongated shape beneath the receiver. The fore-end appears reinforced and the overall design is compact, likely intended for close-quarters use. The combination of wood and metal materials gives it a rugged, utilitarian appearance suitable for urban environments.
description: A compact, double-barrel shotgun with a vented metal receiver and a wooden stock, featuring a hexagonal muzzle and squared trigger guard.
description_view_0: Front view showing the hexagonal muzzle with two visible barrels and the top of the wooden stock.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the vented metal receiver, wooden stock, and the squared trigger guard beneath the receiver.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the end of the wooden stock and the back of the receiver, with the dual barrels visible at the far end.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the same features as view 1, with clear visibility of the trigger guard and the wood grain on the stock.
attribute_barrel_configuration: over-under double-barrel with hexagonal muzzle and vented receiver sides
attribute_trigger_guard_design: elongated, squared trigger guard with a flat bottom and no finger grooves
